QF.id: VB Concept and Role of Mutual Fund_27

question.text: Mrs. Devi invested in a mutual fund scheme 8 months ago. She wants to redeem her units now, but the scheme has a 1% exit load if redeemed within 1 year. If her investment is currently worth Rs. 1,00,000, what will she approximately receive after the exit load?

answer.text: Rs. 1,00,000 — exit load does not apply to senior citizens

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Since Mrs. Devi is redeeming within 1 year (only 8 months), the 1% exit load applies. Exit load = 1% of Rs. 1,00,000 = Rs. 1,000. Amount she receives = Rs. 1,00,000 - Rs. 1,000 = Rs. 99,000. **The Trap:** Exit load applies to all investors regardless of age — there is no special exemption for senior citizens. Rs. 90,000 would be a 10% exit load, which is far too high. She cannot receive more than the current value. **Cross-Exam Context:** This is also tested in Series V-A (MF Distributors) under exit load calculations. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always calculate the exit load impact for clients before they redeem early. Sometimes waiting a few more months to avoid the exit load saves them money. In this case, waiting 4 more months would save Mrs. Devi Rs. 1,000.
